title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. sublatifolia
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. sublatifolia, also known as the incarnate orchid or marsh orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, making it useful in treating conditions such as varicose veins and hemorrhoids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. sublatifolia include its potential to improve circulation, reduce swelling, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders. Dried roots of the plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including cardiovascular and respiratory problems.
